experiences.intentions: The active T piece is near the top in its spawn orientation with an empty board. Early game goal is to place pieces flat and leave flexibility for upcoming J,S,I,O. Candidate actions: moving left or right would shift placement options but there is no urgency yet; rotating could set up a side tuck later but on an empty field the current flat T orientation is already useful. soft_drop advances while preserving future control, while hard_drop commits too early. Best sub-goal is to start descending toward a low, central placement without overcommitting.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is empty except for the active T near the top in spawn orientation. With no immediate threats, the goal is efficient placement setup for upcoming J, S, I, O pieces. Since only one move is allowed now and hard-dropping immediately into an empty field would commit too early near the center, the best sub-goal is to start shifting the T toward a side to preserve the middle. Moving left begins positioning without risk; rotating is unnecessary, and dropping now reduces flexibility.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is empty except for the active T near the top-center in its spawn orientation. With an empty board, the best early-game objective is usually to place pieces cleanly and keep the center/opening flexible for upcoming J, S, I, O. A horizontal T can be moved later, but the strongest immediate progress is to advance it downward quickly since there are no threats or collisions below. Candidate moves left/right only shift setup without urgency; rotation is unnecessary on an empty field; soft_drop advances only one row. hard_drop safely places the T low on the empty board, accelerating toward building a flat stack and preserving future options.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is essentially empty except for a settled T near the bottom center and the active J at the top around columns 4-6. With so much open space, the immediate goal is to place pieces to build a clean, flat stack for upcoming S, I, O, Z pieces. Hard dropping now would commit the J high over the center and create unnecessary unevenness; lateral movement first is better. A good sub-goal is to move the J toward a side so the center remains flexible for future placements and line clears. Among actions, left shifts the J toward the left wall, which is a strong general-purpose placement path for a J on an empty board. Right would also be possible, but left is slightly more natural here given the existing bottom T sits a bit left of center and we can start shaping the left side. Rotation is unnecessary yet, and soft_drop/no_op waste tempo.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is mostly empty. A J piece is active near the top while a T sits centered at the bottom, creating no immediate collision threat. The goal now is efficient placement to keep the stack flat and set up upcoming S, I, O, Z pieces. Since the field is empty, the strongest move is to place the J quickly rather than spend turns shifting; hard dropping in its current horizontal orientation will land it just above the T and start building a low, compact structure without wasting time.
